revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Anakannon Kofun]][[ja:穴観音古墳]]
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name =Anakannon Kofun | native_name =穴観音古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = 穴観音古墳 | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.307667|N|130.905889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Hita, Ōita|en|Hita, Ōita|qid=Q386968|lt=Hita, Ōita|1=Hita, Ōita}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th-7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]|qq=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|18|27.6|N|130|54|21.1|E|qq=}}|qq=}}|qq=}} {{nihongo|'''Anakannon Kofun'''|穴観音古墳||qq=}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Decorated kofun]] [[Kofun]], located in the Kōno neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Hita, Ōita|en|Hita, Ōita|qid=Q386968|lt=Hita|1=Hita, Ōita}} on the island of [[Kyushu]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1933.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139907|title=穴観音古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023|qq=}}</ref> ==Overview== The Anakannon Kofun is located on a plateau on the left bank of the Mikuma River (the upstream portion of the {{ill|Chikugo River|en|Chikugo River|qid=Q954320|lt=Chikugo River|1=Chikugo River}}) that flows through the Hita Basin, and is estimated to have been constructed between the end of the 6th century and the beginning of the 7th century AD. This area is home to a collection of ruins from the {{ill|Japanese Paleolithic|en|Japanese Paleolithic|qid=Q844172|lt=Japanese Paleolithic|1=Japanese Paleolithic}} period to the Kofun period, and is also known as the "Chōjabaru Ruins". This tumulus is thought to belong to the decorative tumulus culture area of the Chikugo River basin, along with the [[Garandoya Kofun]] located about 700 meters to the north and the nearby [[Hōonjiyama Kofun Cluster]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404|qq=}}{{in lang|ja|qq=}}</ref> The tumulus is a circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=}}-style ''kofun'',with a diameter of 10 meters and height of approximately two meters. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a horizontal cave-style stone-lined multi-chamber structure, opening to the south. The existing burial chamber has lost its entrance and has a total length of 7.2 meters. The rear chamber is 3 meters deep and 2.3 meters wide, and the front chamber is 2.6 meters deep and 2 meters wide. The decorations are painted in two colors: red and green, on the back and right walls of the rear chamber, and on the left and right side walls of the front chamber. Among these, the back wall of the rear chamber has geometric patterns of concentric circles and continuous triangular patterns, the right wall has circular patterns and birds, and the left and right side walls of the front chamber have concentric circles, boats, and both right and left hands. It depicts a person with his legs spread apart. The concentric circular pattern on the front chamber and the inner contour of the boat are concave, but this method is not common in this region, and may have been influenced by the tombs of the {{ill|Higo Province|en|Higo Province|qid=Q259705|lt=Higo region|1=Higo Province}}, which have many carved patterns.<ref name="Shiseki"/> In later generations, because a stone [[:en:Kannon Bosatsu|Kannon Bosatsu]] was placed in the back chamber, it came to be called "Anakannon". It is located approximately 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Hita Station|en|Hita Station|qid=Q5871577|lt=Hita Station|1=Hita Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|qid=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Kyūdai Main Line|en|Kyūdai Main Line|qid=Q870569|lt=Kyūdai Main Line|1=Kyūdai Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/> ==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|qid=Q6573294|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)}} ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} ==External links== *[https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/4625 Visit Oita home page]{{in lang|ja|qq=}}*[https://www.city.hita.oita.jp/material/files/group/36/R4kikakutenleaflet.pdf Hita City home page]{{in lang|ja|qq=}}
{{Kofun navbar|qq=}}
[[en:Anakannon Kofun]]
